draftEditing.notes: 15 criteria covering the full option surface: subject, body, body-file, mutual exclusion, to/cc/bcc replace, add-to/remove-to incremental, add-to/to conflict, attach/remove-attach, combined changes, no-options validation, dry-run, and JSON output. All testable via CliRunner + mock_osascript.

delete.notes: 8 criteria covering default-to-Trash, permanent with confirmation, --yes bypass, --yes without --permanent edge case, bulk IDs, --account scoping, dry-run, and human + JSON output. Good safety coverage.

crossCutting.notes: 8 criteria for error handling, CLI help for both commands, architecture adherence, comprehensive test coverage requirements, full test suite pass, and Sprint 1-7 regression.

observations.description: Product spec 3.9 lists stdin as a body source ('--body <text> / --body-file <path> / stdin: replace body'), but no criterion tests stdin body input for drafts edit. The sprint plan scope doesn't explicitly list stdin either, so this is consistent with scoping — but worth noting for completeness. Could be deferred to Sprint 10 polish or added as a low-priority criterion.
observations.recommendation: Acceptable to ship without — stdin support for compose is tested in Sprint 5 and the same codepath would likely be reused. If the generator implements it, the combined-changes test (C-200) is the natural place to verify.

observations.description: Sprint 5 is missing from regression/registry.json (sprints 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7 are present but not 5). The contract correctly lists regressionSprints: [1,2,3,4,5,6,7], and C-218 ('pytest -v' passes all tests) and C-219 (all Sprint 1-7 commands work) provide effective regression coverage for Sprint 5's compose and safety model. This is adequate.
observations.recommendation: No action needed. The evaluator should verify compose/reply/forward tests pass as part of C-218.

observations.description: C-196 tests --add-to and --remove-to but there are no corresponding --add-cc/--remove-cc or --add-bcc/--remove-bcc options. This is consistent with the product spec which only mentions incremental add/remove for the To field. Flagging for awareness — this is by design, not a gap.

safetyReview.notes: The safety notes are thorough and well-aligned with CLAUDE.md guardrails. Key points: (1) no --dangerously-send on the edit command, (2) delete defaults to Trash, (3) --permanent requires confirmation, (4) all permanent delete testing via mocked osascript only, (5) evaluator forbidden from running --permanent against real Mail.app. The out-of-scope list explicitly excludes sending from drafts edit, which is correct.

testabilityAssessment.notes: All 31 criteria specify exact CLI commands, expected behaviors, and verification checks. Test methods are appropriate: unit-test with mock_osascript for AppleScript-touching paths, cli-execution for help text, code-inspection for architecture, test-execution for test suite coverage. Thresholds are binary and unambiguous.

regressionCoverage.notes: All completed sprints listed. C-218 and C-219 provide comprehensive regression via full test suite execution and explicit verification of all prior commands.

outOfScopeReview.notes: Six out-of-scope items are clearly stated and reasonable. Notably: no send from edit, no dedicated drafts list (already available via messages list --mailbox Drafts), no undo/revert for delete, no ambiguous cross-account delete resolution. These are sensible scope boundaries for Sprint 8.
